top_blockers: Non-bootstrap peer replies will not see prior task events
Visible sends are not idempotent or atomic with state events
Raw runtime tools can bypass adapter safety policies
Ambiguous reply matching should fail closed

findings.title: Non-bootstrap peer replies will not see prior task events
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit state hydration contract for every Flow turn before claiming durable reply matching.

findings.title: Visible sends are not idempotent or atomic with state events
findings.recommendation: Use reserve-send-confirm transitions or platform idempotency keys before visible side effects.

findings.title: Raw runtime tools can bypass adapter safety policies
findings.recommendation: Expose a restricted runtime facade; visible writes must go through adapter decisions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On non-bootstrap peer replies, CrewAIFlowStateConverter will not see prior task events unless a new hydration path is added.
A crash after send_message but before task-event recording can duplicate delegation or final synthesis on retry.
If Flow methods can access raw AgentTools, they can bypass join/finalization safety checks by sending visible messages directly.
